name: Gwyllgi
description: The Gwyllgi, also known as the Black Dog of Wales, is a spectral black dog said to haunt lonely roads and paths in the Welsh countryside. It is often described as a terrifying apparition with glowing red eyes, foul breath, and a shaggy coat. The Gwyllgi is a harbinger of death, and its appearance is believed to be an omen of impending doom or misfortune. Some accounts claim it guards treasures or protects the weak, while others paint it as a malevolent spirit that delights in frightening travelers. Its howl is said to be bone-chilling and capable of striking fear into the hearts of even the bravest individuals. Encounters with the Gwyllgi are often brief but unforgettable, leaving a lasting impression of dread and unease. It is a creature deeply rooted in Welsh folklore, embodying the wild and untamed aspects of the land. The Gwyllgi serves as a cautionary tale, reminding people to respect the power of nature and the unseen forces that may dwell within it. Variations in descriptions exist, with some tales emphasizing its immense size and others focusing on its eerie silence before a sudden, terrifying appearance. The creature remains a potent symbol of fear and mystery within Welsh culture.
subtitle: The Omen of Death in Dog Form
appearance: The Gwyllgi is typically depicted as a large, black dog with shaggy fur and glowing red eyes. Its size can vary, but it is often described as being larger than a normal dog, sometimes even as large as a calf. Its breath is said to be foul and its presence radiates an aura of dread. Some accounts mention sharp teeth and claws, while others emphasize its silent movement and sudden appearance.
habitat: Lonely roads, dark forests, mountain passes, and ancient ruins in Wales.
behavior: The Gwyllgi is primarily a solitary creature, often appearing unexpectedly to travelers and then vanishing without a trace. Its behavior is generally considered malevolent, as it is believed to be a harbinger of death and misfortune. However, some stories suggest that it can also act as a guardian, protecting certain places or individuals. Its primary goal appears to be to instill fear and unease.

keyMyths.title: The Crossroads Encounter
keyMyths.description: A traveler met the Gwyllgi at a crossroads and, after a terrifying encounter, learned of an impending tragedy in his village. He was able to warn his community, preventing further loss of life.

sources.author: Marie Trevelyan
sources.work: Folklore and Folk Stories of Wales
sources.description: A collection of Welsh folklore, including stories about the Gwyllgi.
sources.text: A collection of Welsh folklore, including stories about the Gwyllgi.
